extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN Fabio won the 9km straight sprint finish in wet conditions, taking the race lead with bonus seconds over GROENEWEGEN Dylan.
tactical_patterns: Breakaway contested both intermediate sprints hard for bonus seconds, unusual pattern revealing GC tension in short stage race format.
Quick-Step organized lead-out extremely early on final lap, exploiting wet roads to deter rival sprint trains from positioning.
DOULL Owain aggressively hunted intermediate sprint bonus seconds from breakaway, Sky prioritizing GC over stage hunting.

rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: seventh win in rookie season, taking race lead shows ability to execute under pressure in technical wet finishes
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: failed to finish top-3 despite being race leader and stage two winner, sprint form deteriorating
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: neutral: second place confirms consistency but inability to convert against emerging rivals like Jakobsen
rider_signals.WALSCHEID Max: neutral: third place finish shows Sun Web sprint depth but lacks final acceleration against Quick-Step train
rider_signals.VAN HOOYDONCK Nathan: positive: contested intermediate sprints from breakaway aggressively, showing BMC developing classics rider's opportunism
rider_signals.DOULL Owain: positive: won second intermediate sprint from break, Sky using experienced domestique to harvest GC bonus seconds creatively
rider_signals.SÜTTERLIN Jasha: neutral: won first intermediate sprint from break before dropping back, Movistar using breakaway for sprint points harvesting
